title: Aftermath - Let's Play - Introduction
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HDN25GTdn40

Daniel (@dungeondive) opens his Let's Play of **Aftermath**, Jerry Hawthorne's newest beast-epic from Plaid Hat Games — the designer behind Mice and Mystics, Stuffed Fables, and Comanauts. He coins the phrase *critter crawl* on the channel for the first time in this video, and frames Aftermath as the ultimate distillation of what Jerry has been working toward: a post-calamity world where humans have vanished, nature has reclaimed the city, and tiny critters now manage a colony, hunt for scrap and food, and explore the ruins.

The two heroes he picks for the campaign: **Grumpel** the guinea pig (Throw ability, steak knife, personal goal *build a garden*) and **Mesaiya** the mouse (Team Focus, toothpick crossbow, personal goal involving mission card 11 and alliance cards). Win condition is unusual: complete all four campaign-chosen heroes' personal goals — not the *story*. The storybook itself uses a clever multi-use encounter card system; cards reference passages in the book, so a single deck of ~30 encounter cards can resolve to hundreds of unique narrative moments.

Daniel ends with a friendly grumble about Plaid Hat rule books trending too thin — the read-me-first card references an *influence card* that takes determined digging to identify. He suspects this is part of why Comanauts bounced off some players. *Aftermath*, he hopes, will be different.

----

Aftermath's win condition is *complete every chosen character's personal goal*, not *finish the story*. Does that reframe campaign play for you, or does it just feel like a parallel objectives list bolted onto a regular campaign?
